businessModel.text:
Danaher operates as a diversified industrial conglomerate with two primary operating segments, following the separation of its Varex imaging business:
  
    Life Sciences: Provides instruments, consumables, and software for drug discovery, bioprocessing, and clinical diagnostics. Key brands include Beckman Coulter (clinical diagnostics), Pall (filtration and purification), Cytiva (bioprocessing), and Leica Microsystems (microscopy).
    Diagnostics & Biotechnology: The broader biotech tools franchise serves pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies across the drug development lifecycle — from discovery through to clinical manufacturing.
    DBS (Danaher Business System): Danaher's proprietary operational excellence methodology is embedded across all acquisitions, driving consistent margin improvement post-integration — this disciplined approach to M&A and operational leverage is the core of the Danaher investment thesis.
  
  Danaher is a serial acquirer that uses DBS to systematically improve the margins and competitive positioning of acquired businesses before deploying capital into the next platform acquisition. The model has worked extraordinarily well over 20+ years.

thesisEvaluation.text:
Bull Case
      68 / 100
      Biotech funding stabilises and the next wave of GLP-1 and cell/gene therapy drugs drives a multi-year bioprocessing infrastructure buildout, benefiting Cytiva and Pall significantly. DBS drives margin expansion above 28 per cent. New product cycles at Beckman Coulter accelerate consumables revenue. Shares re-rate to 40×+ earnings as growth re-accelerates.
      Weight: 30%
    
    
      Base Case
      52 / 100
      Biopharma CapEx remains subdued throughout 2026, with modest recovery in 2027. DBS continues to drive incremental margin improvement. EPS grows at mid-single-digit rates as share gains offset industry headwinds. The shares trade in a 33–40× earnings range with limited multiple expansion available.
      Weight: 50%
    
    
      Bear Case
      38 / 100
      Biotech funding collapse accelerates as interest rates stay elevated and VC funding dries up further. Pharmaceutical customers cut R&D budgets sharply. Danaher's core diagnostics and life sciences revenues decline materially. DBS cannot offset the revenue contraction and margins fall below 24 per cent.
      Weight: 20%

recommendation.text:
Conviction Score: 53/100 — REDUCE
  While Danaher is a high-quality business with a demonstrated M&A flywheel, the shares trade 21.9 per cent below their 52-week high at a P/E of 37.7×. The biotech spending headwind has not fully resolved, and the near-term earnings growth trajectory does not justify the premium valuation. Hold off on new positions until the biopharma CapEx environment shows clearer recovery signs.
  
    Buy Below$155
    HOLD Zone$155–$215
    Reduce Above$215
    Stop / Sell$145

entryExit.text:
DHR — Danaher Corp (DHR) — Entry / Exit Framework

Reduce / Trim: Above $193.4 — the stock is trading near or above the 52-week high. Capital should be rotated elsewhere.
Tighten or exit on any bounce above $208.57 — the risk/reward is poor at current levels.
Stop-loss (for remaining exposure): $247.66 — a close above the 52-week high would confirm the bull case is breaking down.
Target: $194.43 — partial mean reversion toward the middle of the 52-week range.
